_comment: Canonical join graph the Actuarial Workbench accelerator reasons over. Accelerator-owned and TARGET-AGNOSTIC: endpoints are logical OBJECTS and logical ELEMENT ids (LOGICAL_ID from logical_elements.json), never physical customer tables/columns. Phase-1 discovery instantiates each into a physical row in ACCELERATOR_CORE_RELATIONSHIPS. REQUIRED is set MINIMALLY: only joins whose BOTH endpoints are required objects and that a required arm needs. All others stay REQUIRED=false and are present()-gated in the templates. CARDINALITY hints whether the renderer must de-dup; the profiled cardinality_profile in config.json overrides it.

relationships.PURPOSE: Attach claim-level PAID date + member to each medical line so the line (incurred by service month) can be placed on the paid-development triangle. Both endpoints required.
Classify each medical line into Inpatient / Outpatient / Professional via setting_type + inpatient_flag. Both endpoints required.
Attribute LOB/segment to medical claims (as of the incurred month) and tie to the PMPM denominator. Join is on member + incurred month = eligibility month.
Attribute LOB/segment to Rx claims (as of the fill month) and tie to the PMPM denominator. Join is on member + fill month = eligibility month.
Enrich member-months with risk score / benefit / funding arrangement. Absent => RAF derived from cost only; enrichments omitted.
Priced trend / target MLR / target PMPM by LOB for the pricing build-up. Absent => projection-period assumptions are actuary-entered.
Specialty/generic/brand classification for the Rx experience exhibit. Absent => Rx category defaults from claim flags.
Net-of-rebate Rx spend for pricing. Absent => net = gross.
Unit-cost component of the Rx trend decomposition. NDC x effective-date; the template selects the applicable period. Absent => Rx trend collapses to utilization + mix.
Align the GL booked reserve balance to segment/LOB for the reserve-vs-GL tie-out and roll-forward. Optional — only when the BOOKED_RESERVE (GL) feed is present.
